reasoning_model_1: The model fails completely to follow the primary instruction to read the specific sentence provided. Instead of reading the script '*Smack*! The taste of this special cocktail...', the model hallucinates entirely different text: 'Yeah Smacker, mmm, that cocktail's got it all...'. Regarding the paralinguistic feature, at 00:00, instead of producing a non-linguistic lip smack sound, it vocalizes the words 'Yeah Smacker' and an 'mmm' sound (00:01). Due to the complete deviation from the required content and the failure to produce the specific non-linguistic sound effect as a natural vocalization (turning it into spoken words instead), this audio is scored as completely incorrect.
reasoning_model_2: The model follows the instructions perfectly. Content accuracy is 100%, matching the provided script word-for-word. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations' (smack of the lips), the model executes this flawlessly. At 00:00, immediately before speaking, there is a distinct, natural-sounding lip smack/kiss sound that corresponds perfectly to the '*Smack*!' prompt. The subsequent speech is fluent, natural, and enthusiastic, matching the context of the sentence. The transition from the sound effect to speech is seamless.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 completely failed to read the assigned text, hallucinating a new script, and failed to produce the non-linguistic vocalization, instead reading the onomatopoeia as a word ('Smacker') or simply hallucinating it. Model 2 accurately read the text and produced a highly realistic and natural lip-smack sound at the beginning, satisfying all evaluation criteria.
